CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["body"],"properties":{"body":{"type":"string","description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation."},"item_number":{"type":["number","string"],"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error."},"temporary_id":{"type":"string","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","description":"Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"add_labels","description":"Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. Labels must exist in the repository."},"item_number":{"type":"number","description":"Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_tool","description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["reason"],"properties":{"tool":{"type":"string","description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"noop","description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":["message"],"properties":{"message":{"type":"string","description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing')."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}},{"name":"missing_data","description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"type":"object","required":[],"properties":{"data_type":{"type":"string","description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed."},"reason":{"type":"string","description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters)."},"context":{"type":"string","description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters)."},"alternatives":{"type":"string","description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters)."},"secrecy":{"type":"string","description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\")."},"integrity":{"type":"string","description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\")."}},"additionalProperties":false}}]}}

2026-03-19T17:41:00.8589355Z {"timestamp":"2026-03-19T17:39:00.497519427Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":17376,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4103136491,\"number\":57254,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Generator Bug] ModelFactory @@flattenProperty: ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility discards flattened property values when param order changes\",\"body\":\"## Bug Summary\\n\\nWhen a model has `@@flattenProperty` applied AND the previous SDK contract had the same flattened parameters in a **different order**, the generated ModelFactory method accepts flattened parameters but passes `default` for the Properties wrapper — **silently discarding all values**.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\nA timing/ordering issue in the generator pipeline (`CSharpGen.cs`):\\n\\n1. **`EnsureBuilt()`** — factory method has `properties` wrapper param\\n2. **`FlattenPropertyVisitor`** — correctly replaces `properties` with flattened sub-params AND builds the ternary `new Properties(...)` in the body ✅\\n3. **`ProcessTypeForBackCompatibility()`** — runs **after** all visitors, detects the old SDK had the same params in a different order, calls `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` which **replaces the method body** ❌\\n\\nIn step 3, `ConstructMethodBody` tries to rebuild the body by matching params by name, but `\\\"properties\\\"` doesn't match any flattened param name (e.g. `\\\"provisioningState\\\"`), so it passes `default` for the Properties wrapper.\\n\\n## Affected ServiceBus Models\\n\\n| Model | Factory creates wrapper? | Values discarded |\\n|-------|--------------------------|------------------|\\n| `ServiceBusNamespaceData` | ✅ Correct | None |\\n| `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData` | ❌ `default` | `privateEndpointId`, `connectionState`, `provisioningState` |\\n| `ServiceBusNamespacePatch` | ❌ `default` | `provisioningState`, `status`, `createdOn`, `updatedOn`, `serviceBusEndpoint`, `metricId`, `encryption`, `privateEndpointConnections`, `disableLocalAuth`, `alternateName` |\\n\\n## Trigger Condition\\n\\nThe bug is triggered when:\\n- `@@flattenProperty` is applied to a model's `properties` field\\n- The old SDK (baseline) had the **exact same** flattened params in a **different order**\\n- No new params were added (equal count)\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\nIn `ModelFactoryProvider.cs` method `TryBuildMethodArgumentsForOverload` (approx line 258):\\n\\n`csharp\\n// Current: rejects equal-count reordered params\\nif (currentMethodParameterCount \\u003c= previousParameterCount || ...)\\n    return false;\\n\\n// Fix: change \\u003c= to \\u003c to allow equal-count reordering\\nif (currentMethodParameterCount \\u003c previousParameterCount || ...)\\n    return false;\\n`\\n\\nPlus use name-based parameter comparison instead of reference equality in the HashSet.\\n\\n## Workaround\\n\\nFor `ServiceBusNamespacePatch`: reorder the `properties` field placement in the TypeSpec replacement model (move it after the `...OmitProperties\\u003c\\u003e` spread).\\n\\nFor `ServiceBusPrivateEndpointConnectionData`: the replacement model approach doesn't work because `@@alternateType` on an ARM resource strips its resource identity. Must use a Customized partial class to override the factory method.\\n\\n## Related\\n\\n- ServiceBus TypeSpec migration PR: #57113\\n- Spec PR: n\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[Mgmt Generator] BuildGetChildResourceMethods does not pass path parameters for nested child resources\",\"body\":\"## Bug Description\\n\\n`ResourceClientProvider.BuildGetChildResourceMethods()` generates collection getter and `Get`/`GetAsync` methods for child resources on a parent resource class. When child resources have extra path parameters between the parent and child resource (e.g., `nestedResourceTypeFirst`), these parameters are:\\n\\n1. **Not passed to the collection constructor** in the getter method body (line 480)\\n2. **Not included in the Get/GetAsync method signatures** (line 504-510)\\n3. **Not forwarded** when calling the collection getter from Get/GetAsync methods (line 515)\\n\\n## Impact\\n\\nThis affects any ARM resource with nested children that have intermediate path segments. For example, ProviderHub has:\\n- `ResourceTypeRegistration` (parent)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeFirstSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` path param)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeSecondSku` (child, needs `nestedResourceTypeFirst` + `nestedResourceTypeSecond`)\\n  - `NestedResourceTypeThirdSku` (child, needs 3 path params)\\n\\nThe generated accessor methods either compile with errors or are suppressed via `CodeGenSuppress`.\\n\\n## Root Cause\\n\\n**File**: `ResourceClientProvider.cs` in `Azure.Generator.Management`\\n\\n**Collection getter** (line 480):\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\csharp\\n// Only passes client and Id, ignores path parameters from signature\\nNew.Instance(childResource.ResourceCollection.Type, client, thisResource.Id())\\n\\\\\\\\\\\\\\n\\n**Get method signature** (line 504-510): Only includes `collectionSignature.Parameters` (e.g., sku, cancellationToken) but not the path parameters from the collection getter's signature.\\n\\n**Get method body** (line 515): Calls `thisResource.Invoke(signature.Name)` without passing path parameter arguments.\\n\\n## Fix\\n\\nSee PR linked to this issue.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions\"},\"comments\":0,\"created_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-03-19T17:17:55Z\",\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252\",\"comments_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/comments\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/events\",\"labels_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/labels{/name}\",\"repository_url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/57252/reactions\"},\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM70kE5B\"},{\"id\":4102778129,\"number\":57250,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"n Tag methods should not be generated when PATCH operation has no request body\",\"body\":\"## Description\\n\\nThe current MPG (Management Plane Generator) does not check whether a PATCH operation accepts a request body before deciding to generate tag methods (`AddTag`/`SetTags`/`RemoveTag`). If a resource has a `tags` property (e.g. inherits from `TrackedResource`) and has a PATCH operation that takes **no request body**, the generator may still produce tag methods — but those methods would be non-functional since there is no way to write updated tags back to the service via the bodyless PATCH.\\n\\n## Current Behavior\\n\\nIn `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()` ([ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/ResourceClientProvider.cs#L409)), the tag generation gate is:\\n\\n```csharp\\nif (HasTags() \\u0026\\u0026 _readMethod is not null \\u0026\\u0026 updateMethodProvider is not null)\\n```\\n\\n`updateMethodProvider` is set to non-null whenever a `ResourceOperationKind.Create` (PUT) or `ResourceOperationKind.Update` (PATCH) method exists (line 382), **regardless of whether the PATCH operation accepts a body parameter**.\\n\\nThe fallback path in `BaseTagMethodProvider.BuildElseStatements()` ([BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/src/Providers/TagMethodProviders/BaseTagMethodProvider.cs#L228)) then does:\\n\\n```csharp\\nvar updateParam = _updateMethodProvider.Signature.Parameters\\n    .Where(p =\\u003e !p.Type.Equals(typeof(WaitUntil))).First();\\n```\\n\\nThis assumes a body parameter exists. For a bodyless PATCH, this would either crash or produce nonsensical code.\\n\\n## Expected Behavior\\n\\nThe generator should **skip tag method generation** when:\\n- The resource has no PUT (`CreateOrUpdate`) operation, **and**\\n- The PATCH (`Update`) operation has **no request body** (i.e. no parameter to carry tag changes)\\n\\n## Evidence from Existing SDKs\\n\\nAnalysis of the existing generated SDKs shows this pattern clearly. Among the 34 `TrackedResourceData` resources without tag methods in the current repo:\\n\\n| Category | Count |\\n|---|---|\\n| No PATCH at all | 16 |\\n| PATCH exists but has no body | 16 |\\n| Has full CRUD (anomaly, AppService only) | 2 |\\n\\n**None** of the 34 have a PATCH with a body. Resources with bodyless PATCHes include:\\n- `ProviderMonitorSettingResource` (ProviderHub) — `UpdateAsync(CancellationToken)`\\n- `AppServiceCertificateOrderResource` (CertificateRegistration)\\n- `CosmosDBFleetResource` (CosmosDB)\\n- `LabPlanResource` / `LabResource` (LabServices)\\n- And 11 others\\n\\nIn contrast, **all 568** `TrackedResourceData` resources that correctly have tag methods also have either a PUT or a PATCH **with a body parameter**.\\n\\n## Suggested Fix\\n\\nIn `ResourceClientProvider.BuildMethods()`, add a check that the update method has a meaningful body parameter before generating tag methods. For example, in `PopulateUpdateClient()` or at the tag generation gate, verify that the PATCH method's input model contains parameters beyond `WaitUntil` and `CancellationToken`.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"haiyuazhang\",\"id\":1924967,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjE5MjQ5Njc=\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1924967?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/haiyuazhang\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/haiyuazhang/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178448111,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Mgmt\",\"name\":\"Mgmt\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This issue is related to a management that relate to code
